proven.claim: "Filing N issues" — N is a sat-only count. collectViolationIssues filters
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 518
; "Filing N issues" — N is a sat-only count. collectViolationIssues filters
; verifications to z3Result === "sat"; unsat (proven) results are excluded.

(declare-const total_verifications Int)
(declare-const sat_count Int)
(declare-const unsat_count Int)
(declare-const issues_length Int)

; Code transition: fakeResults includes both proven (unsat) and violations (sat)
(assert (>= total_verifications 0))
(assert (>= sat_count 0))
(assert (>= unsat_count 0))
(assert (= total_verifications (+ sat_count unsat_count)))

; Code transition: collectViolationIssues filters to sat only
(assert (= issues_length sat_count))

; Negate: could issues_length equal total (include unsat)?
(assert (> unsat_count 0))
(assert (= issues_length total_verifications))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: "Filing N issues" overcounts when issues[] contains duplicate titles.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 518
; "Filing N issues" overcounts when issues[] contains duplicate titles.
; buildIssue() produces the same title for same principle+claim+file+line.
; Two sat violations across different contracts can produce identical titles.
; fileViolationIssues deduplicates via `seen` Set → those become skipped.
; The log at 518 claims N will be filed, but only unique_title_count are acted upon.

(declare-const issues_length Int)
(declare-const unique_title_count Int)
(declare-const dup_count Int)

; Code transition: collectViolationIssues builds one IssueData per sat verification;
; same principle+extracted_claim+filePath+line → identical title string
(assert (> issues_length 1))
(assert (> dup_count 0))
(assert (> unique_title_count 0))
(assert (= issues_length (+ unique_title_count dup_count)))

; Code transition: fileViolationIssues will skip dup_count items (seen.has branch)
; Violation: "Filing N" overstates; only unique_title_count will be processed
(assert (< unique_title_count issues_length))
(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 518
; fileIssues calls result.derivation.contracts.map(...)
; result is typed { derivation: any } — `contracts` field is completely unchecked.
; If contracts is null or undefined, .map() throws TypeError BEFORE line 518 executes.

(declare-const contracts_is_array Int)   ; 1 = valid array, 0 = null/undefined/non-array
(declare-const map_throws Int)           ; 1 = TypeError thrown, 0 = proceeds

; Code transition: result.derivation typed `any`; no null/array guard before .map()
(assert (= contracts_is_array 0))

; Code transition: calling .map() on non-array throws TypeError
(assert (=> (= contracts_is_array 0) (= map_throws 1)))
(assert (= map_throws 1))

; Violation: crash before line 518 is reached
(check-sat)
